Name: JARS-Experimental
LongName: JARS-Quant Table 2: Reporting Standards for Studies With an Experimental Manipulation
Description: The JARS-Quant experimental table provides psychology-specific reporting expectations for studies with an experimental manipulation, including randomized and nonrandomized assignments and additional clinical-trial reporting modules.
Aim: To ensure psychology experiments and related trials report manipulations, allocation, masking, flow, fidelity, analytic methods, and trial-specific protocol details clearly enough for evaluation and replication.

Scope.Coverage: Psychology and behavioral-science studies with an experimental manipulation, including randomized and nonrandomized allocation and trials involving clinical or health-related interventions.
Scope.Focus: Manipulation details, delivery and analysis units, participant flow, treatment fidelity, baseline data, adverse events, randomization or assignment procedures, masking, and clinical-trial registration and protocol reporting.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not for purely observational designs, longitudinal studies without manipulation, or research syntheses. This table is meant to be used together with the JARS-Quant master table.

primary.description: Appelbaum M, Cooper H, Kline RB, Mayo-Wilson E, Nezu AM, Rao SM. Journal Article Reporting Standards for Quantitative Research in Psychology: The APA Publications and Communications Board Task Force Report. American Psychologist. 2018. PMID: 29345484.

items.item_name: Describe manipulations and delivery in replicable detail
items.item_question: Does the manuscript report the content, delivery, setting, duration, personnel, materials, compliance activities, and procedural detail needed to replicate each experimental condition?
items.item_question_background: Readers must understand not just that an intervention or manipulation occurred, but exactly what happened, who delivered it, where, and for how long.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the content of each manipulation, delivery method, materials, deliverer qualifications, setting, intended dosage or duration, and any procedural manual or access information.

source_page_refs.quote: Provide details of the experimental manipulation(s) intended for each study condition ... Sufficient detail to allow for replication.

items.item_name: Report units of delivery, analysis, and participant flow
items.item_question: Does the manuscript identify the unit of delivery and analysis and report participant or group flow through the study, including attrition and inclusion in primary analyses?
items.item_question_background: Experiments often involve grouped delivery or clustered assignment, so readers need to know both how the intervention was delivered and what unit actually entered the analysis.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the unit of delivery, unit of analysis, any analytic adjustment for unit mismatch, and the reported participant or group flow through study stages.

source_page_refs.quote: State the unit of delivery ... Describe the smallest unit that was analyzed ... Report the total number of groups ... and the number of participants assigned to each group.
source_page_refs.locator: Table 2 units and participant flow rows

items.item_name: Report fidelity, baseline data, adverse events, and mechanism
items.item_question: Does the manuscript report treatment fidelity, baseline characteristics, adverse events, and discussion of the intended mechanism and implementation barriers?
items.item_question_background: Interpreting experimental results depends on knowing whether the manipulation was delivered as intended, how groups looked at baseline, and whether harms or implementation barriers emerged.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract fidelity evidence, baseline demographic or clinical characteristics, adverse events, and discussion of causal pathways or implementation barriers.

source_page_refs.quote: Provide evidence on whether the experimental manipulation was implemented as intended ... Describe baseline demographic and clinical characteristics ... Report all important adverse events or side effects.

items.item_question: For randomized experiments, does the manuscript report the unit of randomization, sequence generation, concealment, masking, and statistical methods used to compare groups?
items.item_question_background: The randomized-assignment module adds allocation transparency requirements parallel to other trial-reporting standards while staying tailored to psychology experiments.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the randomization unit, sequence-generation method, concealment details, masking procedures, and primary comparison methods.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the unit of randomization and the procedure used to generate the random assignment sequence ... State whether and how the sequence was concealed.

items.item_name: Report nonrandom assignment and bias-minimization procedures
items.item_question: For nonrandomized experiments, does the manuscript report the assignment method, restrictions, bias-minimization procedures, masking, and adjusted statistical analyses?
items.item_question_background: When assignment is not random, transparent reporting of how assignment occurred and how selection bias was addressed becomes especially important.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the unit of assignment, assignment method, any blocking or minimization, matching or propensity-score procedures, masking, and statistical adjustment strategy.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the method used to assign units to study conditions ... State procedures employed to help minimize selection bias.

items.item_name: Report trial registration and protocol transparency for clinical trials
items.item_question: For clinical trials, does the manuscript state registration status and number, describe the protocol's availability and changes, and report trial-specific participant, assessor, and intervention details?
items.item_question_background: The clinical-trial module extends the experimental table with registration and protocol transparency requirements that reflect modern trial expectations.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract trial registration status and number, protocol availability, protocol changes, assessor blinding, multisite details, and trial-specific intervention tailoring or materials information.

source_page_refs.quote: State whether trial was registered prior to implementation ... Report whether the study protocol was publicly available.

items.review_reason: The available page extract truncates some Module C rows; verify full wording if precise trial item granularity is needed.

Aliases: Reporting Standards for Studies With an Experimental Manipulation
EvidenceExamplesPositive: A psychology experiment reports manipulation delivery, unit of randomization, masking, participant flow, and treatment fidelity under APA reporting standards.
A behavioral clinical trial reports registration, protocol availability, assessor masking, and trial-specific intervention details.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: A nonmanipulated correlational study should use the observational JARS module instead of the experimental one.
A meta-analysis with no experimental manipulation is not a JARS-Experimental match.
Matching.Explain: Use this overlay for psychology manuscripts with an experimental manipulation, including randomized, nonrandomized, and clinical-trial variants.
